name: case_5_cross_page_empty_next_ps
description:
1a is on page 1, 1b on page 2. The VL call for page 2 could not see the original stem and returned parent_stem='' for 1b (this is the documented CROSS-PAGE-PARENT-STEM behavior from the VL prompt). 1a.qt has trailing bridging. Since 1b.parent_stem is empty, rescue falls back to group_stems['1'] which was populated in Step 1 from 1a's own ps. The group_stems canonical 'earliest page, longest' value here is from 1a itself, so it does NOT contain the bridging — rescue will fail to match and conservatively leave 1a.qt alone.

EDGE-CASE NOTE: this fixture demonstrates that when the cross-page fix has not yet propagated the stem to the continuation-page item, rescue cannot help. That's the correct behavior — rescue must never guess. A follow-up run after _attach_parent_stems Step 2 (which fills empty ps with group canonical) would then give rescue another try, but rescue runs BEFORE Step 2 by design (to decouple from group-max-overwrite). Accepting this trade-off.

pre_items.parent_stem: The equation of a circle with centre C is x^2 + y^2 - 8x + 4y - 5 = 0.
pre_items.question_text: Find the radius of the circle and the coordinates of C. The point P(1, 2) lies on the circle.
Show that the equation of the tangent to the circle at P is 4y = 3x + 5.

group_stems.1: The equation of a circle with centre C is x^2 + y^2 - 8x + 4y - 5 = 0.

expected_post_rescue.question_text: Find the radius of the circle and the coordinates of C. The point P(1, 2) lies on the circle.
expected_post_rescue.parent_stem: The equation of a circle with centre C is x^2 + y^2 - 8x + 4y - 5 = 0.

expected_post_rescue.question_text: Show that the equation of the tangent to the circle at P is 4y = 3x + 5.
